questions.question: The chain installed by Jahangir outside his palace for hearing grievances.
The Abyssinian Prime Minister who kept Ahmadnagar independent of Mughal control.
The fort that surrendered to Jahangir on November 16, 1620.
The first Englishman sent to Jahangir's court in 1608 by the East India Company.
The only Mughal empress who had coins minted with her name.
The rebel leader from whose clutches Nur Jahan rescued Jahangir in 1626.
The jewel-encrusted golden throne commissioned by Shah Jahan.
The method of decoration involving inlay of semi-precious stones in marble used in Shah Jahan's buildings.
The title taken by Aurangzeb meaning 'Conqueror of the World'.
The ninth Sikh Guru who was executed by Aurangzeb in Chandni Chowk in 1675.
The powerful military force organised by Guru Gobind Singh.
The treaty signed by Shivaji in 1665 after the siege of Purandhar.
questions.source_section: Rise of the Marathas and Chhatrapati Shivaji

questions.question: The Bijapur general killed by Shivaji in 1659.
questions.source_section: Rise of the Marathas and Chhatrapati Shivaji

questions.question: The last Mughal ruler who ruled in Delhi.
The Persian invader who sacked Delhi in 1739 and stole the Peacock Throne.
